steps.title: Translate the phrase into an expression
steps.content: "8 times a number x" is $8x$. "3 more than" means add 3, giving $8x + 3$.

steps.title: Set equal to 83
steps.content: The full statement "is equal to 83" gives: $$8x + 3 = 83$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm
steps.content:
• A: $(3)(8)x = 83$ simplifies to $24x = 83$ — this is 24 times x, not 3 more than 8 times x. Eliminate A.
  • B: $8x = 83 + 3$ puts the 3 on the wrong side, giving $8x = 86$. Eliminate B.
  • C: $3x + 8 = 83$ swaps the roles of 3 and 8 — this is "8 more than 3 times x." Eliminate C.
  • D: $8x + 3 = 83$ is exactly "3 more than 8 times x equals 83." That matches choice D — D is correct.
